translated: The wall was so high... no, no, no, that's not it.
Everyone is so ambitious and shares that same mindset,
which is why I feel inspired to work harder myself.
I might not have felt that way if I were alone,
but seeing and feeling everyone's performances made me think, "Maybe I can go further,"
or "Maybe there's more I can do."
We really were inspiring each other with our solo songs.
I felt it too. We all did.
I was really happy about that.
Even though a solo song is for one person, I felt like I wasn't alone during that performance.
It felt like we were all building it together.
I've never worn anything like that before.
If you called it "street clothes," it's probably the closest to what I'd actually wear.
It's simple and stylish enough that you wouldn't look out of place walking around town.
Some people might actually wear that.
That was a first for me.
I've never worn fishnets in my life.
Is it okay for people to see you like that?
I just felt grateful for the opportunity.
We were practically worshipping you backstage.
I was honored to be worshipped.
Oh, and during the final show, I think...
It wasn't part of the choreography, but your hair
got in your face. You brushed it away while looking forward or down...
I can't quite remember which, but that gesture
was caught perfectly by the camera.
It was just too good. Like, wow.
I'd definitely watch that on repeat.
But it was so beautiful, I loved it.
Some people might already be talking about it.
Please try to remember it, everyone.
Moving on, this is from Liella Name: Hajiketa Kata Popcorn.
All the 2nd and 3rd generation members were saying this,
the choreography was incredibly difficult. It's the kind of difficulty
that's hard for the audience to imagine. From the outside, it looks
like a fun and light dance, but it was actually incredibly tough.
When we first learned the moves, we all felt like our souls were about to leave our bodies.
We were all worried if we could actually sing and dance it at the same time, but as we practiced, we got in sync, and that anxiety turned into enjoyment.
So on the day of the live, instead of being worried, I felt like I could enjoy that sense of unity with the audience. What about you?
